g.text: The cost curve of AI feature development bends.
From 'linear in features' to 'linear in systems integrated.'
MCP (Model Context Protocol) — an emerging open standard for connecting AI agents to enterprise systems. Like USB for AI integrations.
Marginal cost to ship the next feature (illustrative)
This gap is the standardisation dividend.
Cost scales with systems, not features.
New systems are additive, not multiplicative.
MCP doesn't just make features cheaper — it expands what's buildable.
Some features are economically infeasible without standardised integration. Under MCP they pencil — that is the bigger executive story.
